name: Asparagus Cashew Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------SAUCE-----------------------------------
3 tablespoons Soy sauce
1 teaspoon (preferably toasted) Sesame Oil
2 tablespoons Cornstarch
1/4 teaspoon Dry crushed red pepper
1 1/2 cups Water or vegetable stock
1 tablespoon Minced Gingerroot
----------------------------------ND STEP----------------------------------
2 tablespoons Safflower oil
1 sweet red pepper, chopped
1 pound Fresh Asparagus (woody parts of stems removed, tender part cut into 3-inch lengths - approximately 3 cups)
1 teaspoon Garlic, minced
4 scallions, chopped
1 cup Cashews (dry-roasted and unsalted, or raw slivered almonds)
GARNISH: Mandarin orange sections and toasted sesame seeds, optional
Cook the brown rice.
In a small bowl, combine soy sauce and cornstarch. Stir in remaining sauce ingredients; set aside.
In a wok or large skillet, heat oil. Stir-fry asparagus, scallions, pepper, and garlic until vegetables are crisp-tender.
Stir sauce mixture; pour it over the vegetables and stir until it is thickened and bubbly. Reduce heat; fold in cashews. Cover and cook 1 minute, until cashews are heated through. Serves 4-6.
VARIATIONS: - 3-4 cups broccoli florets may be substituted for asparagus; or
